findings.description: Header layout differs - PDF has a side-by-side layout with seal/logo on left and official names on right, HTML stacks them vertically
findings.evidence: PDF: Logo and 'NC DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES' are side by side with Governor/Secretary/Deputy info in a right-aligned box. HTML: Logo is above, then names listed below in bold left-aligned text

findings.description: Governor, Secretary, and Deputy Secretary names are bold in HTML but displayed in a different typographic style in the PDF header box
findings.evidence: PDF: Names appear in a bordered box with specific formatting. HTML: Names appear as bold standalone lines without the box styling

findings.description: Footer layout simplified in HTML - location, mailing address, and contact info removed from bottom of page 1 but appear at top of page 2
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 has full footer with address, phone, fax. HTML page 1 footer appears minimal, but the info shows at top of page 2 HTML rendering

findings.description: Footer contact information appears at top of page 2 in HTML instead of at bottom of page 1
findings.evidence: PDF: Footer with location/mailing/web/phone info is at bottom of page 1. HTML: This info appears at top of page 2 rendering

findings.description: Table header formatting differs slightly - PDF uses gray shading on header rows, HTML uses plain styling, but data and structure are preserved
findings.evidence: PDF tables have gray-shaded header rows. HTML tables have plain headers. All data values match.

findings.description: Solvency Review Defensive Interval table header row label differs slightly
findings.evidence: PDF: 'Month' and 'LME-MCO' appear as separate header labels. HTML: Shows 'Month LME-MCO' combined. Structure and data are otherwise identical.

findings.description: No h1 heading exists in the HTML document. All table section titles use h3 directly with no h1 or h2 headings present, creating a broken heading hierarchy.
findings.evidence: The HTML uses only <h3> tags for section headings like 'Claims Accuracy and Timeliness Review: Summary Findings', 'Solvency Review: Current Ratio Summary Findings', etc. There is no h1 document title or h2 headings.

findings.description: The Claims Accuracy table has duplicated column headers instead of properly structured Result/Finding sub-columns. Each month's data pair (value + compliance) is represented as two identically-named columns.
findings.evidence: HTML thead has: 'Timeliness of Provider Payment (Within 30 days)' repeated twice, 'Claims Processing Accuracy' repeated twice, 'Financial Accuracy' repeated twice. The PDF uses 'Result' and 'Finding' as sub-headers. Similarly, the Solvency tables have month names duplicated (e.g., 'March', 'March') instead of using value/compliant sub-columns.

findings.description: The entire letter content (page 1) appears to be duplicated in the HTML. The letter body, signature block, header information, and footer are all rendered twice - once in an apparent first rendering and then again in a second main element.
findings.evidence: The HTML contains the full letter text starting with 'January 16, 2025' and ending with 'Attachments' inside a <main> element, but the outer document also has a <main> wrapper. The header with JOSH STEIN, DEVDUTTA SANGVAI, JAY LUDLAM appears once in the header section and the footer information (NC MEDICAID, address, phone) appears in the page-footer div - this is acceptable as header/footer consolidation. However, there are multiple <main> elements in the document which is structurally problematic.
